Replan incorporating review findings — 12 plans became 13
Deep code review + fixes (2 blockers, 4 warnings, 1 info)
CI green including VM integration suite (60 passed, 5 skipped)
User review of executed phase — 5 decisions corrected in CONTEXT.md

remaining_tasks.name: Replan phase 2 for the corrected decisions (delta only, not a full replan)
Human UAT: questionary/Live TUI rendering check

human_actions_pending.action: Run the questionary/Live TUI rendering check
human_actions_pending.context: Unit tests stub the prompt, so nothing proves questionary's checkbox renders, takes the terminal from a paused Live panel, and hands it back. Driver was at /tmp/pcswitcher-review-spike.py and will not survive a reboot; it is regenerated by plan 02-02's task. Defer until after the rework, since the review call sites change.

human_actions_pending.action: Read docs/configuration.md cold and confirm a user could enable a package job, predict what happens to their target, and mark a package machine-specific
human_actions_pending.context: Deferred human checkpoint from plan 02-12. Deferred again until after the doc migration (D-33), which moves job explanations out of configuration.md.

decisions.decision: D-24 corrected: batched review is PER MANAGER, not across managers
decisions.rationale: The user never asked for a cross-manager review. The discuss-phase wrote 'one batched review ... grouped by manager', which was read as cross-manager and contradicted D-15's independent jobs. The PackagePhaseCoordinator built to satisfy it must be removed.

decisions.decision: D-15 corrected: four jobs, adding manual_installs_sync
decisions.rationale: Unreproducible-item detection and the snippet registry were folded into apt_sync, so disabling apt_sync silently disabled manual-install detection. Half that work (unowned files under /usr/local and /opt) is not apt's business at all.

decisions.decision: D-21 corrected: skip-once is a valid resolution
decisions.rationale: The job raised PackageItemFailures unless every unreproducible item had a snippet or a skip-always record. Skip-once is a real decision — the user may be declining something temporary — and must not make the run unclean.

decisions.decision: D-23 corrected: manual_installs_sync pushes package-snippets.yaml itself via send_file
decisions.rationale: config_sync runs at SyncStep 9, before any review, so it cannot carry a snippet the user has not authored yet. folder_sync cannot be relied on either — user sync jobs are user-controllable, so no job's correctness may depend on another running.

decisions.decision: D-30 added: apt collateral classified auto vs manual before refusing
decisions.rationale: Blanket refusal of any install whose apt-get -s simulation removes something blocks legitimate installs whose only collateral is an auto-installed dependency. Manual collateral becomes a reviewable item (install-anyway / skip / abort), asked at plan time so no prompt appears mid-apply.

decisions.decision: D-31/D-32/D-33 added: jobs/packages/ layout, no empty config sections, job docs out of configuration.md
decisions.rationale: jobs/ should hold job modules plus base.py and context.py only. Empty placeholder config sections are YAGNI noise. configuration.md explains configuration; what a job does belongs in its own doc, including for pre-existing jobs.

decisions.decision: Replan phase 2 in place rather than inserting phase 2.1
decisions.rationale: Nothing has merged — PR #206 is still open — so the delta never becomes visible history and one coherent phase 2 lands on main. phase_status is 'Needs Review', which GSD's closed-phase gate treats as legitimately replannable without --force.

next_action: Run /gsd-plan-phase 2 with the verbatim prompt embedded in .planning/phases/02-package-management-sync/.continue-here.md under <next_action>. Plan the delta only — 13 plans shipped and their work stands.
context_notes: Phase 2 is implemented, code-reviewed and CI-green on branch gsd/phase-02-package-management-sync (PR #206, open, marked ready for review). The rework is not defect-driven: the implementation faithfully executed decisions that were wrong. CONTEXT.md is now corrected and is the source of truth; the code has not been touched yet. The corrected CONTEXT carries an 'Already Delivered — Do Not Undo' section listing the hard-won fixes that must survive replanning.
